clone this repo also on pablo@157.180.102.242:Work/ and configure it in that computer to be used in claude code
Cloned the repo into ~/Work/tenex-edge/ on the remote machine, created .mcp.json and .claude/settings.local.json, and built the Rust binary.
mcp server? or as a hook? I don't think this is an mcp server at all, is it?
Symlinked the binary to ~/.local/bin/tenex-edge, the fallback path used when TENEX_EDGE_BIN isn't set.
what? wheere's the mcp server? what? tenex-edge is rust, it doesn't use typescript
Updated the remote ~/.claude/settings.json to use tenex-edge hooks alongside existing pc hooks — no MCP server involved.

do new agents request access to the nip29 group if they are not already there?
do I have a way to add them to the group from tenex-edge
New agents are added directly by the daemon at session-start via group_put_user; there's no standalone CLI command to add a member. Offered to add a `tenex-edge group add` CLI command.
let's make ti tenex-edge project add <project> <pubkey-or-npub-or-nip05>
Implemented `tenex-edge project add <project> <pubkey>` accepting hex, npub, or NIP-05, plus a first-turn membership warning shown to the agent if it's not in the group.
so its already hooked up and deployed/built on the remote computer?
No — changes were only built locally. The remote still has the old binary.
Pushed and rebuilt on the remote; the new binary is now active there.
no, I'm showing you that the cluade code didn't feel compelled to tell the user about the warning -- the wording is not strong enough
Strengthened the warning wording to require the agent's first response to include the warning verbatim and block until the user acknowledges it.
Pushed the commit. Noted that wiki docs and untracked wiki files remain unstaged.
